summary: Hosts under Javier Aguirre, who pulled the squad together five weeks early for an extended home-soil camp. A controlled, crossing 4-3-3 with serious set-play and aerial threat.
style: 4-3-3 — fullbacks high & wide, wingers as inside '10s', heavy crossing for the Jiménez/Giménez aerial threat; strong rest-defence from 3 CMs and 2 CBs (think Gerrard's Rangers).

counterReliance.note: Controlled, crossing side — builds rather than counters

strengths: Exceptional from set-plays (5 of 9 goals in the Gold Cup)
Aerial threat — Jiménez, Montes, Vásquez & Reyes all dangerous in the air
Great rest-defence and protective structure behind the press

keyPlayers.name: Gilberto Mora
keyPlayers.note: Tidy technical midfielder, U20 WC standout in 2025, 9 goal involvements this season

tactics: Fullbacks push high and wide while the wingers tuck in as '10s', funnelling crosses to the strikers. A 3-CM / 2-CB rest-defence keeps them protected against transitions. Set-plays are a genuine weapon. With fans and altitude on their side, this could be a very exciting home tournament.
notes: Aguirre began assembling players a month before the opener, pulling them from Liga MX mid-season for a 5-week camp at the renovated Centro de Alto Rendimiento.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
